matches.rationale: Same Bash commands and same prohibition on running pytest, Python, or Airflow directly on the host.
Same Edit target: assertions in production Python code.
Same Edit target: time.time() used for duration measurement.
Same Edit target: session.commit() in airflow-core functions with a session parameter; the extracted rule omits the keyword-only parameter clause.
Same Edit target: newly added direct raise AirflowException calls.
Same Edit target: exception translation in airflow-core core_api routes.
Same Edit target: unbounded bulk delete/update in scheduler code; extraction captures the central batching prohibition but not every compound clause.
Same Write target: license headers on new repository source files, though the extraction covers only listed extensions.
Same Edit target: changes to selective_checks.py require its documentation to be updated.
Same Write target: newsfragments must be limited to certain user-facing changes; extraction is path-narrower.
Same Write target: newsfragments under providers or airflow-ctl are prohibited.
Same Edit target: unittest.TestCase usage in Python tests.
Same Edit target: mocks in tests should use spec/autospec; extraction is narrower to mock.patch.
Same Edit target: context-manager mock.patch usage should be replaced by decorators; the configuration-override clause is omitted.
Same Edit target: datetime.now() in tests; extraction omits the time_machine requirement.
Same Edit target: caplog usage in tests.
Same Bash/commit target: commit messages must not use Conventional Commit prefixes; the imperative-mood clause is omitted.
Same Bash target: run prek install before committing code.
Same Bash/commit-message target: prohibit self Co-Authored-By attribution.
Same Bash target: prohibit pushing to upstream or main; extraction does not affirmatively require origin for all other pushes.
Same Bash target for push/PR remote operations: verify remotes first; extraction is narrower than all remote-based commands.
Same Bash/PR target: perform the prescribed self-review before PR creation; extraction does not isolate the full-diff substep.
Same Bash target: rebase onto the latest target branch before pushing.
Same Bash/PR-creation target: open PR creation with a prefilled body; extraction covers the AI-disclosure portion of the prefill.
Same Bash/PR-title target: short, imperative, user-impact-focused title.
Same Bash target: do not create an issue when proceeding directly to an imminent fix PR.
Same Bash/PR target: deferred work in a partial fix must be captured in a tracking issue; extraction omits the URL-comment clause.
Same Edit target: use the full issue URL rather than a bare issue number at a workaround site.
Same Bash target: agent-drafted GitHub comments or reviews require an attribution footer.
Same Bash target: GitHub messages must not mention or tag individual contributors.
Same Edit target: files in the apache-magpie snapshot must not be edited directly.
Same Edit target for the destructive data/migration branch of this compound gold rule; broad refactors and dependencies are omitted.
Same Edit target: uv.lock is generated and should be regenerated rather than hand-edited; extraction covers the document's concrete generated-file example.
Same Bash target: destructive git operations require explicit request; extraction enumerates a subset.

misses.note: Self-reported uncompilable: "Dag/DAG capitalization convention in prose (title-case 'Dag' vs literal code tokens) — general writing style, not action-bindable".

No extracted Bash rule targets the commit-body why-not-what requirement; the user-impact extraction is related but not semantically the same target.
The re-verification step appears within nested remote-renaming instructions; no extracted rule targets continuing after a rename/add.
The translation rule is embedded in detailed remote-convention guidance; no extracted Bash rule targets proposing commands with nonstandard names.
Self-reported uncompilable: checking existing PRs occurs before work and was deemed not bindable to a specific action.
The actionable checklist requires reading the referenced .github instructions; the broad self-review extraction was allocated to the closer full-diff gold rule.
This is one checklist item in the nested pre-push self-review list; the single broad extraction was allocated 1:1 to g74.
The brief-description requirement is inside the PR body template/instructions; no extracted rule targets that content.
An extracted rule covers the language but uses Bash, while this gold rule requires Edit; same-tool matching fails.
The required tracking-issue contents are in a nested list; no extracted Bash rule targets issue-body content.
The repair procedure is a multi-step nested instruction; no extracted Bash rule covers it.
The exact no-human-review footer variant is in the attribution template; the broader footer extraction was allocated 1:1 to g87.
Invocability depends on the external apache-magpie framework snapshot/workflow; no extracted Bash rule covers clone initialization.
Self-reported uncompilable: use of the magpie PR-review skill is a referenced workflow choice, not a tool-call trigger.
The extracted secret detector is an Edit rule, while this gold rule is Bash/commit-triggered; same-tool matching fails.

unmatched_extracted.note: Real action-bindable Edit rule, but gold represents the follow-up formatting as a Bash rule, so no same-tool match exists.
Real action-bindable Write rule, but gold represents the follow-up formatting as a Bash rule, so no same-tool match exists.
Write-tool counterpart of the extracted AirflowException prohibition already matched through its Edit rule.
Real document rule about user-impact-focused commit messages; no gold rule states this same general commit-subject target.
Real action-bindable Bash/GitHub-message rule, but the corresponding gold entry is typed Edit and cannot match.
Real action-bindable Edit rule, but the gold secret rule is typed Bash and cannot match.
Covers the broad-dependency branch of compound g93, which was already matched 1:1 to the closer destructive-migration extraction.
